title: podman --remote exec ignores empty detach keys
body: I've confirmed the fix in 5.8.0 for https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/27414 but it only fixes the issue for podman run, not podman exec. In the latter case, an empty string is treated as nothing and the default "C-p,C-q" is used instead of disabling the detach sequence.

comments.body: @jamescassell Hi, I wasn't able to reproduce this on the main branch. Could you provide a complete reproducer for this issue?

A reviewer has determined we need more information to understand the reported issue.  A comment on what is missing should be provided. Be certain you:

* provide an exact reproducer where possible
* verify you have provided all relevant information - minimum is `podman info`
* answer any follow up questions

If no response to the `needs-info` is provided in 30 days, this issue may be closed by our stale bot.

For more information on reporting issues on this repository, consult our [issue guide](ISSUE.md).

On MacOS.

podman run --privileged -d registry.access.redhat.com/ubi10-init

podman exec --detach-keys '' -it d09 bash

press Ctrl-P,Ctrl-Q and observe disconnect


podman version
Client:        Podman Engine
Version:       5.8.0
API Version:   5.8.0
Go Version:    go1.25.7 X:nodwarf5
Git Commit:    37bfeded1f9df9577b138cbad90a5da95fdf9b89
Built:         Wed Feb 11 19:00:00 2026
Build Origin:  Copr: packit/containers-podman-28079
OS/Arch:       linux/arm64
I'm looking at the code, and it seems to be a classic issue with uninitialized values and default settings during JSON marshaling and unmarshaling.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
